items.title: RT by @WhiteHouse: BREAKING: VP Vance announces major progress made by the United States and its negotiating team while finalizing a deal with Iran: “Yesterday was a very, very good day. We made a lot of good progress. We did exactly what we wanted to do… the Iranians have agreed to invite IAEA inspectors back into their country. That is a major milestone for the American people. And the first step in permanently denuclearizing- permanently ending a nuclear weapons program in Iran.”
items.summary:

items.title: Christine Lagarde: Hearing of the Committee on Economic and Monetary Affairs of the European Parliament
Vance Hails ‘Good Day’ of Iran Talks as US Waives Oil Sanctions
items.summary: US Vice President JD Vance said talks with Iran over the weekend were “very, very good,” as the sides attempt to reach a peace deal within two months and formally end a war that’s reverberated through the global economy.

items.title: FACT FOCUS : Netanyahu claims about Iran nuclear program run counter to public evidence – Winnipeg Free Press
Vance says Iran will allow nuclear inspectors back into the country
items.summary: The US vice-president says a great deal of progress has been made after the first round of talks between the US and Iran.

items.title: Gulf Oil Floods Through Hormuz at Fastest Pace Since War Began
items.summary: The flow of oil through the Strait of Hormuz is running at the fastest pace since the Iran war began — despite Tehran stating that the world’s key shipping chokepoint is shut and a report that the Islamic Republic continues to harass passing vessels.

items.title: Lagarde: To ensure inflation stabilises at our 2% medium-term target, we decided to raise the three key ECB interest rates by 25 basis points at our June meeting. With this decision, we remain well positioned to navigate the uncertainty caused by the war.
items.summary:
Lagarde: To ensure inflation stabilises at our 2% medium-term target, we decided to raise the three key ECB interest rates by 25 basis points at our June meeting.

With this decision, we remain well positioned to navigate the uncertainty caused by the war.

items.title: Lagarde: The decision to raise rates is robust across the scenarios prepared by staff, meaning that in all scenarios a rate hike is warranted. Recent developments have remained within the range of scenarios considered.
items.summary:
Lagarde: The decision to raise rates is robust across the scenarios prepared by staff, meaning that in all scenarios a rate hike is warranted.

Recent developments have remained within the range of scenarios considered.

items.title: Foundation Has Been Set to End Iran War, Vance Says
items.summary: US Vice President JD Vance says "we laid a very good foundation for a successful final deal." He spoke to reporters in Switzerland after holding talks with Iranian negotiators.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: President Christine @Lagarde: The euro area economy was gaining some traction when the war in the Middle East broke out. Real GDP rose by 0.3%, quarter on quarter, in the first quarter of 2026, once adjusted for exceptional volatility in Ireland.
items.summary:
President Christine @Lagarde: The euro area economy was gaining some traction when the war in the Middle East broke out.

Real GDP rose by 0.3%, quarter on quarter, in the first quarter of 2026, once adjusted for exceptional volatility in Ireland.

items.title: Lagarde: The war is now weighing on activity, and incoming information points to a slowdown, especially in services. Inflation rose to 3.2% in May from 3.0% in April. The main driver of inflation since February has been rising energy inflation, which was above 10% in April and May.
items.summary: Lagarde: The war is now weighing on activity, and incoming information points to a slowdown, especially in services. Inflation rose to 3.2% in May from 3.0% in April. The main driver of inflation since February has been rising energy inflation, which was above 10% in April and May.

items.title: Lagarde: Inflation expectations over shorter horizons are well above the levels seen before the outbreak of the war in the Middle East. At the same time, most measures of longer-term inflation expectations stand at around 2%.
items.summary:
Lagarde: Inflation expectations over shorter horizons are well above the levels seen before the outbreak of the war in the Middle East.

At the same time, most measures of longer-term inflation expectations stand at around 2%.

items.title: Lagarde Sees No Need for More Forceful ECB Response to War
items.summary: The European Central Bank doesn’t need to react more forcefully to the fallout from the Middle East conflict because inflation is set to return to target over the medium term, President Christine Lagarde said.

items.summary: In a sharp reversal of American policy, the Treasury announced a 60-day reprieve, permitting the sale of Iranian crude as part of the preliminary U.S.-Iran deal toward ending the war.

items.title: Explosion at Qatar Gas Plant Kills at Least 13 and Injures 66
items.summary: The blast happened at a key site for natural gas production. The authorities blamed a technical malfunction while operations were being restarted after the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: Mideast Live Updates: Vance Points to Progress After First Round of U.S.-Iran Talks
items.summary: Vice President JD Vance said Iran had agreed to invite U.N. nuclear inspectors back into the country in the first round of negotiations that ended Monday. Iran did not immediately comment.

items.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer stepped down as leader of the governing Labour Party. Andy Burnham, the party’s most popular politician, said he would seek the prime minister’s job and secured the support of a potential rival.
